def build_output(src):
    patched = patch_source(src)
    col = [x for x, _ in MAC if x in set(ids(patched))]
    if col:
        die("macro collision: " + ",".join(col))
    u = patched.find("using namespace std;")
    if u < 0:
        die("missing using namespace std")
    patched = patched[:u] + "".join("#define %s %s\n" % (a, b) for a, b in MAC) + patched[u:]
    out = transform(patched)
    if out.count("main(){") != 1:
        die("post-transform main anchor lost")
    if "K3C::run()" not in out:
        die("post-transform K3C call lost")
    if "X92::run()" in out or "MVI92::run()" in out or "VGC92::run()" in out:
        die("forbidden stale route call present after transform")
    return out

def compile_and_gate(outp):
    if not shutil.which("g++"):
        die("g++ not found")
    exe = outp.with_suffix("")
    cmd = ["g++", "-std=c++17", "-O2", "-pipe", str(outp), "-o", str(exe)]
    subprocess.run(cmd, check=True)
    print("compile=" + " ".join(cmd))
    run_exe = str(exe)
    if not os.path.isabs(run_exe) and os.sep not in run_exe:
        run_exe = "." + os.sep + run_exe
    ans = subprocess.check_output([run_exe], input=OFFICIAL_SAMPLE, timeout=12)
    first = ans.splitlines()[0].decode("ascii", "replace") if ans else ""
    print("sample_first_line=" + first)
    if first.strip() != "8 12":
        die("official sample first line mismatch; expected 8 12")
    print("sample_gate=OK")

def main():
    ap = argparse.ArgumentParser()
    ap.add_argument("src", nargs="?", default=None)
    ap.add_argument("-o", "--out", default=DEFAULT_OUT)
    ap.add_argument("--allow-sha-mismatch", action="store_true")
    ap.add_argument("--no-compile", action="store_true")
    args = ap.parse_args()

    inp, expected_sha = choose_src(args.src)
    raw = inp.read_bytes()
    sh = bsha(raw)
    if expected_sha and sh != expected_sha and not args.allow_sha_mismatch:
        die("base blob sha mismatch got %s expected %s for %s" % (sh, expected_sha, inp))

    src = raw.decode("utf-8")
    out = build_output(src)
    n = len(out.encode("utf-8"))
    if n > LIMIT:
        die("generated source over limit: %d>%d" % (n, LIMIT))

    outp = Path(args.out)
    outp.write_text(out, encoding="utf-8", newline="")
    print("base=" + str(inp))
    print("base_blob_sha=" + sh)
    print("output=" + str(outp))
    print("output_bytes=%d" % n)
    print("source_limit=%d" % LIMIT)
    print("route=K3C exact case3/case5 cubemap support reconstruction after current-best final state")
    print("fallback=exact current-best output unless AF+strong_validator+vps512 and smaller vertex count pass")
    if not args.no_compile:
        compile_and_gate(outp)

if __name__ == "__main__":
    main()

Important contest update. We are stuck at 81.938904/100 on Kattis simplifygeometry; the public top target is 91.80+. Many recent submissions compile, pass sample, and get all 7 cases accepted, but they only TIE 81.938904, so small edits around the current best are not enough. One attempted variant regressed to 81.614991. We need a real +10 point breakthrough, not another 0.0/0.3 tweak.

Use everything already attached in this chat: problem_b_transcription.md, baseline.cpp, baseline.py, simplifygeometry.zip, the camera/evaluator images, and the GitHub repo history: https://github.com/lequoctran181/IMC_Challenge_2026 . The key clarification remains: symmetric Hausdorff is computed over vertices only, not over surface interiors. Current tests are final. Output must be a valid closed triangular manifold, first line V F, C++17, output <=100 MiB, and the official sample must start with exactly 8 12.

Your task is deliberately broad: independently re-think the whole problem and IMPLEMENT a submit-ready artifact that can plausibly jump directly from 81.94 to 91.80+. Prioritize hidden-test specialization, mesh-family detection, evaluator exploitation, vertex-Hausdorff loopholes, exact manifold-preserving remeshes, and any unconventional strategy that can cut vertex counts much more while keeping SSIM >=0.9. Do not merely wrap or rename the known best solution. If you modify the known best, add a validator/rollback so the fallback remains exact-best quality.

You must think deeply and use Pro Extended capacity. Do not stop after a quick 20-30 minute shallow answer. If your idea is not strong enough for 91.80+, keep searching. Return a concrete artifact only: either (A) one complete C++17 submission file, or (B) one complete Python generator that reads the current best C++ source and writes a new C++17 candidate. The final answer must contain the full code in a single fenced code block, plus only the minimal compile/sample commands. No prose-only answer, no pseudocode, no partial patch.
